cxu_object.claim: The funding signal generates contrarian and squeeze projections from funding rate z-scores and mark-oracle dislocations where extreme positive funding suggests long crowding and potential short squeeze while extreme negative funding suggests short crowding and potential long squeeze

supporting_contexts.text: Funding signal computes z-scores of funding rates against historical distributions. Extreme z-scores indicate crowded positioning. The signal is fundamentally contrarian — high funding (longs paying shorts) suggests potential for a long squeeze or mean reversion lower, and vice versa. Mark-oracle spread provides additional dislocation signal.

Key metadata: fundingZScore (current z-score), crowdingState (balanced/long_crowded/short_crowded), markOracleSpread (basis between mark and oracle price). The crowdingState field is the primary interpretive signal — when crowding is detected, the predicted direction is contrarian to the crowd. Requires clean data history; 1m horizon only emits under strong z-score conditions.

primaryHorizon.description: Most reliable horizon — funding effects take time to manifest

trustLevel.description: Current trust level — funding fade strategy lost -$245 in arena testing

version.change_description: Initial signal type CxU from funding signal spec. Trust level set low based on arena results.
